20.reason: [Adding '\0' to a character is incorrect; this corrupts the letter. Should assign 'c' directly, then convert case separately.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Adding '\0' to a character is incorrect; this corrupts the letter. Should assign 'c' directly, then convert case separately.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrectly adding '\0' to character; should just assign c. This corrupts the character value.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Adding '\0' to a character is incorrect; this corrupts the letter. Should assign 'c' directly, then convert case.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Adding '\0' to a character is incorrect; it overwrites the letter with a null terminator. Should assign 'c' directly, then convert case if needed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  Adding '\0' to a character is incorrect; this corrupts the character. Should assign c directly, then convert case.

23.reason: [Because temp[h] was already corrupted by adding '\0', case conversion is applied to wrong value.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Because temp[h] was already corrupted by adding '\0', case conversion is applied to wrong value.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Because temp[h] was already corrupted by adding '\0', this conversion is incorrect. Should convert c before storing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Because temp[h] was already corrupted by adding '\0', case conversion is applied to wrong value.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Because temp[h] was set to '\0' above, this becomes '\0' - ('A'-'a'), which is negative and wrong. The case conversion logic is applied after corrupting the character.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  This conversion is applied after adding '\0', which is wrong. Also, 'A'-'a' is negative; should be c - 'A' + 'a'.

32.reason: [Breaks after first non-letter character, so only reads one word from the entire file. Should reset and continue processing rest of file.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Breaks after first non-letter character, so only reads one word from the entire file. Should reset and continue processing rest of file.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Breaks after first non-letter character, so only reads one word from the file instead of all words.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Breaks after first non-letter, so only reads one word from the entire file. Should reset and continue reading the rest of the file.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [This breaks after the first non-letter character, so only the first word in the file is ever read. The function returns immediately after reading one word, ignoring the rest of the file.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  Breaks after first non-letter, so only reads one word total, not all words in file.

67.reason: [Unnecessarily allocates new node 'r'; should just use a pointer to traverse list, not allocate memory. This causes memory leak and logic error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Unnecessarily allocates new node 'r'; should just use a pointer to traverse list, not allocate memory. This causes memory leak and logic error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Unnecessarily allocates a new node 'r'; should just use a pointer without malloc. This causes memory leak and logic error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Unnecessary malloc: 'r' is used as a traversal pointer, not a new node. This leaks memory and causes logic errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Unnecessary malloc: 'r' is used as a traversal pointer, not a new node. This leaks memory and causes undefined behavior when r is reassigned to head->next.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  Unnecessary malloc; r should just be a pointer to traverse, not a new node.
